questions.question: Explain the difference between friend functions and member functions in C++.
expectedPoints: Friend functions are declared with friend keyword inside class but defined outside
Member functions are part of the class and defined inside/outside with scope resolution
Friend functions don't have 'this' pointer, member functions do
Friendship must be explicitly granted, member access is inherent

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of using friend functions and classes in C++ programming. Provide examples where friend functions are particularly useful.
expectedPoints: Advantage: Allows controlled access to private members without breaking encapsulation completely
Advantage: Useful for operator overloading when left operand isn't class object
Disadvantage: Reduces encapsulation and may lead to tighter coupling
Disadvantage: Can make code harder to maintain if overused
Example: Overloading << operator for output streams
Example: Implementing mathematical operations between different classes
Example: Factory pattern implementation where factory needs access to private constructors
